articles.title: AAMA Executive Vice President Pete Gustafson Resigns After 10 Years
articles.summary: The American Amusement Machine Association announced that Executive Vice President Pete Gustafson has resigned effective June 28, 2026, after a decade of leading the trade group. The AAMA board has accepted his resignation and a leadership transition is underway. This is a significant change for the coin-op and arcade industry's primary trade association, and operators and suppliers should watch for updates on new leadership direction.

articles.title: Sony Pictures Makes $100M Strategic Investment in Immersive Venue Tech Company Cosm
articles.summary: Sony Pictures Entertainment has acquired a minority stake in Cosm, a location-based entertainment technology company specializing in immersive dome experiences, injecting $100 million to fuel expansion. Sony's chairman will join Cosm's board, signaling major corporate confidence in the LBE sector. For FEC operators and investors, this deal underscores the growing capital flowing into experiential entertainment and could accelerate Cosm's rollout of venue-scale immersive attractions.

articles.title: Fun Spot America Permanently Closes Atlanta Location in August 2026
articles.summary: Fun Spot America announced the permanent closure of its Atlanta park, with the final day of operation set for August 2, 2026. CEO John Arie Jr. called it an extremely difficult decision for the family-owned chain. The closure is a noteworthy market signal for FEC operators tracking the viability of standalone amusement parks in suburban markets.

articles.title: Hasbro-Branded Family Entertainment Center Coming to Pigeon Forge in Summer 2027
articles.summary: A new Hasbro-branded family entertainment center is slated to open in Pigeon Forge, Tennessee, in summer 2027, bringing major IP-driven entertainment to one of the country's top family tourism markets. The project highlights the growing trend of toy and media brands licensing into the FEC space. Operators should note the competitive implications for nearby venues and the continued appeal of IP partnerships in attracting families.

articles.title: DIVR Labs to Debut New VR Game 'Castle Crusade' for Bowling Centers at Bowl Expo 2026
articles.summary: DIVR Labs will showcase Castle Crusade, a new multiplayer game for its self-service InstaVR platform, at Bowl Expo 2026 in Nashville from June 28 to July 2. The InstaVR system requires no dedicated staff, making it an attractive add-on revenue option for bowling centers and other FECs looking to reduce labor costs while enhancing guest engagement. Operators attending Bowl Expo can demo the attraction at Booth #875.

articles.title: Semnox Solutions Deploys Cashless and Self-Service Tech at New Maryland FEC K&K Adventure Zone
articles.summary: K&K Adventure Zone, a new multi-attraction indoor entertainment center in Prince Frederick, Maryland, has deployed Semnox's Parafait platform including LuminOS game readers, self-service kiosks, and a centralized POS system in partnership with Betson Enterprises. The setup streamlines guest transactions and enables cashless gameplay across arcade, laser tag, VR, mini-golf, and laser maze attractions. This is a strong real-world case study for FEC operators evaluating integrated technology platforms for new builds or retrofits.

articles.title: Tornado Terry's Family Amusement Center in Fort Worth to Close After Three Decades
articles.summary: Fort Worth's Tornado Terry's Family Amusement Center has announced it will shut down after roughly 30 years in operation. The closure of a long-running family-owned FEC reflects ongoing pressures facing independent operators in an increasingly competitive entertainment market. The story serves as a cautionary example for FEC owners navigating rising costs and shifting consumer preferences.

articles.title: Chance Rides Emphasizes Long-Term Service and Support as Key to Maximizing Ride Investment
articles.summary: Ride manufacturer Chance Rides is spotlighting its long-term maintenance, parts availability, and technical support programs as critical factors in helping operators get the most out of their ride investments over years and decades. The company argues that the real measure of a ride's value is its operational longevity, not just opening day performance. FEC and park operators should consider after-sale support capabilities as a major factor when evaluating ride purchases.

articles.title: Star Factory Lantern Promotes Day-to-Night Dinosaur Attraction Strategy to Boost Dwell Time and Revenue
articles.summary: Star Factory Lantern is pitching a dual-mode dinosaur experience concept that combines daytime animatronic exploration with illuminated nighttime displays, designed to extend guest dwell time and increase secondary spending for parks and attractions. The approach targets family venues looking to maximize per-guest revenue without major infrastructure investment. FEC operators with outdoor or large indoor spaces may find this a viable seasonal or permanent revenue add-on.

articles.title: Slick City Action Park Opens at Potomac Mills Mall in Northern Virginia
articles.summary: Slick City Action Park, an indoor slide and active entertainment concept, has opened a new location at the Potomac Mills mall in Northern Virginia. The opening continues Slick City's expansion into large retail mall spaces, a format increasingly attractive to FEC operators seeking anchor tenants or co-tenants in high-traffic shopping centers. This is a relevant competitive and expansion data point for operators in the Mid-Atlantic region.

articles.title: Kokomo Joe's FEC Adding Multi-Level Go-Kart Track
articles.summary: Kokomo Joe's family entertainment center is investing in a multi-level go-kart attraction, signaling continued operator confidence in go-karts as a premium anchor draw for FECs. Multi-level tracks command higher price points and create a more memorable guest experience than flat tracks. This expansion is a useful benchmark for operators considering go-kart upgrades or new track installations.

articles.title: Indoor Slide and Air Court Park Coming to Overland Park, Kansas This Summer
articles.summary: A new indoor slide and air court park is set to open in Overland Park, Kansas, adding another active-entertainment venue to the growing Midwest FEC market. The concept merges slide-based attractions with trampoline-style air courts, reflecting strong consumer demand for physical, family-friendly indoor experiences. Local and regional FEC operators should monitor this opening as a competitive development.

articles.title: Big Air Indoor Trampoline Park Opens New Location in Glendale, Arizona
articles.summary: Big Air, a national indoor trampoline park chain, opened its Glendale, Arizona location this weekend, continuing the brand's geographic expansion across the Sun Belt. The opening adds competitive pressure to existing FECs in the Phoenix metro area. Trampoline park operators nationally should track Big Air's rollout pace as a signal of sector growth momentum.

articles.title: PICO Partners with STYLY to Expand LBE VR Content Distribution Globally
articles.summary: PICO and STYLY have entered a long-term partnership positioning STYLY's spatial platform as a global distribution hub for location-based VR experiences, with PICO 4 Ultra Enterprise headsets selected for their ease of use across all visitor age groups. The partnership focuses on enabling non-technical staff to manage VR attractions reliably, a key operational concern for FECs. This development is relevant for operators evaluating VR headset platforms and content sourcing for new or existing LBE attractions.

articles.title: Wavegarden Reports Surf Lagoons Delivering Up to 9x Equity Multiples and 10x Land Value Gains
articles.summary: Surf technology company Wavegarden has published analysis showing its surf lagoon projects are generating project IRRs as high as 178% and land-value increases of up to 10x across the US and globally. These figures position surf lagoons as high-return investment opportunities within the broader attractions development sector. FEC investors and developers exploring premium experiential attractions should consider the financial performance data as part of their feasibility research.

articles.title: New Adventure Park Billed as 'Like No Other in the Country' Opens in Atlanta
articles.summary: A new adventure park has opened in Atlanta, with operators claiming the concept is unique in the US market. The opening comes in the same market where Fun Spot America is closing, potentially indicating a shift in the type of entertainment experiences gaining traction with Atlanta-area consumers. FEC operators and investors tracking the Atlanta market should note the changing competitive landscape.

articles.title: Chalk & Steel Co. to Open New Indoor Entertainment Venue in Keller, Texas
articles.summary: Chalk & Steel Co. is bringing a new indoor entertainment venue to Keller, Texas, expanding the DFW market's growing roster of family entertainment options. The Keller opening reflects continued suburban Texas demand for experiential entertainment destinations. Operators in the region should assess the competitive implications and potential demand for differentiated FEC offerings.

articles.title: Lululand Adventure Park Announces Pennsylvania Debut for Next Year
articles.summary: Lululand Adventure Park is set to make its Pennsylvania debut, signaling the brand's continued multi-state expansion in the indoor adventure park segment. The opening will introduce a new competitive option for families in the PA market. FEC operators in the region should begin tracking Lululand's concept and pricing model ahead of its launch.

articles.title: UK Heatwave Drives Surge in Demand for Indoor Entertainment Venues
articles.summary: Rising temperatures across the UK are pushing consumers toward indoor entertainment options, creating a short-term demand spike for FECs and indoor attractions. The trend mirrors patterns seen in the US Sun Belt, where extreme heat has historically boosted indoor venue traffic during summer months. FEC operators in warm-weather markets should consider weather-driven demand in their summer marketing and staffing strategies.

articles.title: Splish Splash Water Park Hosts Early-Access Morning for Autism Society Families
articles.summary: New York's Splish Splash water park is hosting a special early-opening event for the Nassau/Suffolk Chapter of the Autism Society of America, providing two hours of low-stress, crowd-free access before regular guests arrive. The initiative is a strong example of inclusive programming that builds community goodwill and broadens a venue's customer base. FEC operators should consider similar sensory-friendly or early-access programs as part of their community engagement strategy.

articles.title: High Tide VR Near Bethany Beach Offers Location-Based VR Entertainment to Coastal Visitors
articles.summary: High Tide VR, located near Bethany Beach, Delaware, is attracting tourists seeking alternative entertainment to outdoor beach activities, positioning itself as a weather-independent option in a seasonal resort market. The venue illustrates how LBE VR attractions can thrive in tourist corridors by capturing visitors looking for novel indoor experiences. FEC operators in resort or vacation markets may find this a compelling model for diversifying their attraction mix.

articles.title: London-Origin Social Entertainment Venue with Virtual Soccer and Interactive Games Opens in Paris
articles.summary: A London-based indoor entertainment concept featuring virtual soccer, interactive games, cocktails, and fast food has opened its first Paris location, demonstrating the cross-border expansion potential of social gaming venues. The concept blends competitive socializing with food and beverage, a format gaining momentum globally among young adult audiences. FEC operators looking to attract 21-plus demographics should monitor this hybrid bar-and-gaming venue format.

articles.title: Masti Zone Opens New Family Entertainment Destination in Greater Noida West, India
articles.summary: Masti Zone has launched a new family entertainment destination at PKS Town Central in Greater Noida West, expanding the indoor FEC market in one of India's fastest-growing suburban regions. The opening reflects strong international demand for FEC concepts beyond Western markets. Suppliers and investors tracking global FEC expansion should note India as an increasingly active development market.

articles.title: ProFun Academy Partners with Ellis & Associates on Aquatic Safety and Attractions Operations Training
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia-based ProFun Academy has announced a strategic training partnership with Safety Skills Training DMCC, the international division of Ellis & Associates, a leading authority in aquatic safety and attractions operations. The partnership will expand access to internationally recognized safety and lifeguard training programs across the Middle East attractions sector. FEC and waterpark operators globally should consider this a reminder of the importance of credentialed safety training programs for staff.

articles.title: Europa-Park's Immersive Dark Ride Dining Restaurant Eatrenalin Earns First Michelin Star
articles.summary: Eatrenalin, the multi-sensory fine-dining restaurant at Europa-Park where guests ride dark ride vehicles through an 8-course meal, has been awarded its first Michelin star by the Michelin Guide Germany. The achievement validates the concept of combining premium food and beverage with immersive attraction-style technology. FEC operators invested in elevated F&B experiences should take note of how experiential dining can generate significant prestige and revenue.

articles.title: New Family Entertainment Facility Set to Open in Springfield, Illinois in July
articles.summary: A new entertainment facility is scheduled to open in Springfield, Illinois, in July 2026, adding a new FEC option to the central Illinois market. Details on attractions and operators are limited, but the opening reflects continued new-venue development activity across mid-sized US markets. Local competitors and suppliers in the region should monitor the launch.

articles.title: ICEE and Coca-Cola Launch Summer 'Win the Weekend' Promotion Tied to America's 250th Anniversary
articles.summary: ICEE is partnering with Coca-Cola on a 'Win the Weekend and Drink in America' summer promotion running June 29 through August 16, offering fans prizes tied to America's 250th anniversary celebration. The promotion is relevant to FEC operators that carry ICEE products, as it provides built-in marketing support during the peak summer season. Operators should coordinate with their ICEE distributor to take full advantage of the campaign's point-of-sale and promotional materials.
